behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher recursion behavior (root vs nested paths) plus daemon handoff from OS event to pipeline. A mocked watcher can hide missing recursive subscription or path normalization bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem/daemon logic, with no AI/ML decision-making or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon process against temp input/output dirs. Create `input/sub/a.txt` after startup. Assert one transformed output appears in output dir and logs/metrics show exactly one processing run for the nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is module handoff: event intake -> dedupe cache/window config -> enqueue decision. This can be caught in-process by wiring those modules with a fake clock; real OS/network/subprocess is not required by the behavior text. Rejected System: text says "processing request" and "same file path within a configurable time window," which can be exercised without real filesystem events.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate intake + dedupe + queue modules with a controllable clock. Submit the same canonical file path twice within the configured window; assert first request enqueues and second is dropped. Advance clock past window and assert a third request enqueues.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS event semantics (duplicate notify bursts). In-process fakes typically emit idealized events and can miss coalescing/debounce race failures. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent chain in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory. Perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., create + append + rename, or editor-style temp-file swap). Verify exactly one pipeline execution/output for that logical file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS/filesystem lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/recreation, watcher invalidation/rebind) that only appears with real watcher primitives and process runtime state. Mocking can hide re-subscription bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior is infra resilience, not AI/ML correctness or multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching `input/`. Remove `input/` while daemon stays alive, recreate it, then create a new file in recreated dir. Assert daemon processes that new file without restart and does not require manual reconfiguration.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file metadata/timestamps and concurrent arrival events; OS timestamp resolution and watcher delivery order can cause real failures that mocks smooth out. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML-driven decision quality or multi-step agent pipeline is described.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon on a real temp input dir. Create multiple files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation-time skew. Assert processing order matches creation timestamps (and define deterministic tie-break if timestamps equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode depends on real write-in-progress behavior (partial content visibility, event timing, possible rename/flush patterns) and watcher timing. In-process fakes often deliver fully written content and miss this race. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML output or chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, open a file in input dir and write it in chunks with delays before close/fsync. Ensure arrival event occurs early. Assert daemon does not process until write completes, then processes once using final full content.
